input.initialPrompt: Analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for this project. I haven't run any coverage yet.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for this project. They haven't run any coverage yet, so I need to first understand the project structure, then set up and run tests with coverage, and finally analyze the results.

Let me start by exploring the project structure.

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a full picture of the project. Let me run the tests with code coverage enabled using coverlet, then analyze the results.

The test project already has `coverlet.collector` as a dependency. I can run tests with coverage collection using `dotnet test --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage"` which will produce a Cobertura XML report.

Let me run the tests with coverage.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-905cfe24b88b4fb0b2ff08d54496d705 && dotnet test --collect:\"XPlat Code Coverage\" --results-directory ./TestResults --no-pager 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with code coverage", "initial_wait": 60}
